messages.content: Build a file-handle resource in Effect-TS v3 using Effect.acquireRelease and Effect.scoped — open a file, read it, then guarantee it closes on success or failure.
What's the Effect v3 way to guarantee a database connection is released on success and failure? Show a minimal example.
Implement a scoped HTTP client in Effect v3 with strict types — acquireRelease, typed errors, no any.
Explain briefly how Effect.scoped works in Effect v3, then show a typed temp-directory resource.
Build a scoped lock in Effect v3 that acquires a mutex token on entry and releases on exit using Effect.acquireRelease.
Convert this try/finally resource pattern to Effect v3 acquireRelease + scoped: `const h = open(); try { use(h); } finally { close(h); }`
Add an Effect.retry (3 attempts) around the inner use step of this scoped DB program — keep the release guarantee: a program that acquires a connection and queries it.
What's the Effect v3 way to make sure a resource is released even when the outer effect is interrupted? Minimal example.
Build a transactional resource in Effect v3: acquire begins a transaction; release commits on success and rolls back on failure.
Show me the Effect v3 equivalent of JavaScript's `using` / `Symbol.dispose` resource pattern. Brief example.
Implement a scoped worker-pool acquisition in Effect v3 with strict types — no any, explicit error type, pool released on exit.
Write an Effect v3 program that opens two dependent resources (config, then client) and closes them in reverse order. Constraint: one Effect.scoped wrap, typed errors.
Explain briefly how acquireRelease integrates with Effect.scoped, then show a cached-service resource.
What's the Effect v3 way to read lines from a file with deterministic cleanup using Effect.scoped?
Build a scoped WebSocket subscription in Effect v3 — open on acquire, send unsubscribe on release, consume one message inside the scope.
Refactor this Effect v3 program to use Effect.acquireRelease + Effect.scoped instead of manual `Effect.ensuring`: a program that opens a channel, uses it, then runs a close via Effect.ensuring.
Write an Effect v3 program that registers a scheduled task as a scoped resource — registered on acquire, cancelled on release. Constraint: typed error for registration, no any.
Show a minimal Effect v3 example that acquires a config file, reads it, uses it to build a client, and closes both in order.
Explain briefly what happens to finalizers registered via Effect.acquireRelease when the program is interrupted, then show a typed demo.
Add a 100 ms Effect.timeout around this scoped file-read so interruption still runs the finalizer: a scoped program that acquires a reader and reads a line.
Build an Effect v3 program that races a slow fetch against a 200 ms timeout using Effect.race and Effect.sleep — the loser is interrupted.
What's the Effect v3 way to run two effects concurrently with fork and collect both results via Fiber.join? Minimal example.
Implement an Effect v3 fastest-wins selector over three typed providers using Effect.raceAll. Strict types, no any.
Explain briefly how Effect.fork models background fibres in Effect v3, then show a typed background-log example using Fiber.interrupt.
Build an Effect v3 snippet that races a cache lookup against a network fetch — whichever returns first wins.
Convert this Promise.race call to Effect v3 Effect.race with typed errors: `Promise.race([fetchPrimary(), timeoutAfter(150)])`.
Add a 200 ms deadline to this forked task using Effect.race — the task runs on its own fibre and must finish in time: `Effect.fork(doWork())`.
What's the Effect v3 way to fork two independent effects and get both results as a tuple? Minimal example.
Implement an Effect v3 race between two typed failing effects — preserve the union in the error channel. No any.
Show the Effect v3 equivalent of `new Promise` background work plus `.then`, using Effect.fork and Fiber.join. Brief example.

Refactor this Effect.all call to explicit Effect.fork / Fiber.join so the fibres are observable: `Effect.all([a, b], { concurrency: 'unbounded' })` where a and b are two independent Effect<number> values.
Write an Effect v3 program that races two long-running workers but logs when the loser is interrupted. Constraint: no any, use Fiber + race.
Build an Effect v3 fan-out: fire four typed probes in parallel and return the first success via Effect.raceAll.
Explain briefly the difference between Effect.fork and Effect.forkDaemon in Effect v3, then show a typed example of a supervised fork.
What's the Effect v3 way to short-circuit on the first failure of several concurrent effects? Minimal example.
Implement an Effect v3 race where the two branches return different successful types — unify via a tagged union. Strict types.
Add an early-interrupt to this forked poller based on a condition from a sibling fibre. The poller is Effect.forever with a console log each iteration.
Show the Effect v3 equivalent of AbortController-based fetch cancellation using Effect.race against a timeout. Brief example.
Build an Effect v3 program where a background heartbeat runs while a main task executes; the heartbeat is interrupted when main finishes.

Write an Effect v3 program that races three delayed effects of different durations, returns the fastest result, and asserts the slower two were interrupted. Constraint: strict types, typed interruption log.
